description_long: This object is a stylized, rust-colored metal sculpture of a jet airplane mounted on a curved pedestal base. The airplane features a streamlined fuselage with a rounded nose, sharply swept-back wings, and twin vertical stabilizers at the rear. The wings are mid-mounted and exhibit a pronounced sweep, while the tail section is characterized by two vertical fins, giving it a distinctive appearance. The entire sculpture is constructed from textured metal, with a visibly weathered and oxidized surface, suggesting exposure to the elements. The base is a rectangular metal plate, also showing signs of rust and wear, providing stability to the upright display. This object is likely intended as a public art installation or commemorative monument in an urban park or plaza.
description: A rusted metal sculpture of a swept-wing jet airplane mounted on a curved pedestal, with twin vertical stabilizers and a weathered rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the rounded nose, cockpit area, and the full sweep of the wings and twin vertical stabilizers.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, the curvature of the pedestal, and the trailing edges of the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the fuselage, the twin vertical stabilizers, and the pedestal's attachment to the base.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the nose, cockpit, and the right wing's sweep, as well as the pedestal's curve and base.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and twin vertical stabilizers; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the sculpture.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizer tail configuration, resembling an H-tail layout.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible; the airplane is mounted on a pedestal.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the fuselage with no visible canopy or window details.
